summary.intent: Implement task #280: a framework-agnostic Calendar core state machine for the ars-ui Rust component library, covering single- and multi-select, locale-aware grid math, min/max constraints, focus management, and ARIA announcements. Port all implementation-driven spec deviations back into the spec.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented Calendar machine across grid.rs, mod.rs, tests.rs (118 unit + snapshot + 3 proptest + 2 spec-conformance tests). All spec deviations reconciled back into calendar.md. Also added Callback::new_ref to ars-core to support higher-ranked closures over reference args. PR created with cargo xci-fast green.

repo: CalendarDate does not implement Ord; use CalendarDate::compare() for ordering. BTreeSet<CalendarDate> is unusable — requires a SelectedDates newtype wrapping a sorted Vec.
Callback::new requires Args: 'static, blocking use with &T reference arguments. A new Callback::new_ref constructor with HRTB (for<'a> Fn(&'a T) -> Out) was needed and added to ars-core.
ConnectApi pattern uses svc.connect(...) — not Machine::connect(...). The Service object returned from Machine::init is the entry point.
Clippy rejects let _ = svc.send(...); the idiomatic pattern used throughout the codebase is drop(svc.send(...)).
cargo-mutants baseline check fails when pre-existing snapshot mismatches exist in unrelated components (meter/progress); use --baseline=skip to isolate calendar mutation results.
ComponentIds replaces manual heading_id/grid_id string fields — all part IDs derive from ids.part("name") at connect time.
The real ars-i18n API uses u8 + Result<_, CalendarError> for date construction and arithmetic, not NonZero<u8> + infallible calls as written in the original spec.
Spec §1.8 referenced Event::HoverDate/HoverEnd and Context fields is_range/range_start/range_end that were not declared in §1.2 — these were range-calendar drift, not Calendar spec.
cargo insta review is the required workflow for accepting new snapshots; cargo insta test runs tests and generates pending snapshots.

code.finding: Added new_ref constructor: impl<T: ?Sized + 'static, Out: 'static> Callback<dyn for<'a> Fn(&'a T) -> Out + Send + Sync>. This is the first HRTB Callback use in the workspace and required bypassing the existing new() which bounds Args: 'static.

SelectedDates newtype (sorted Vec<CalendarDate> via compare()) is necessary because CalendarDate lacks Ord. Manual Props::PartialEq was eliminated once IsDateUnavailableFn switched from fn pointer to Callback (which derives PartialEq via Arc::ptr_eq).

Pure grid math (weeks_for, month_year_at_offset, ordered_weekdays) is isolated in its own file with inline unit tests. CalendarDate arithmetic returns Result — every call needs explicit error handling even when overflow is structurally impossible.
Calendar proptests appended to the existing date_time proptest file (not a new file). proptest_config() is imported from the common harness.
Spec conformance anatomy tests live in this shared file per component, not in separate files per component.

workflow: Plan mode with AskUserQuestion was used to resolve two scope ambiguities before writing a line of code — this prevented wasted work on hover events and the shift-space anchor design.
Parallel exploration agents (Explore subagent type) were spawned concurrently to read large spec sections and existing component patterns without burning the main context window.
TDD order: write named failing tests first (task 3), then implement the state machine (task 5), then accept snapshots — compile errors from absent types provide a useful scaffold.
Coverage check (cargo xtask coverage) after tests revealed uncovered branches; additional targeted tests lifted mod.rs coverage from ~88% to 95.85% before mutation testing.
Mutation tests on a crate with pre-existing snapshot failures require --baseline=skip; expect 10-20 minutes runtime on a medium-sized module.
cargo xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate; run it before staging changes and creating the PR.
Spec edits belong in the same PR as the implementation when the implementation reveals spec errors — don't defer them to a follow-up.

friction: cargo-mutants baseline kept failing due to pre-existing meter/progress snapshot mismatches unrelated to calendar — wasted multiple invocations before discovering --baseline=skip.
Mutation tests ran for 15+ minutes and had to be killed; results were never fully confirmed (two partial runs showed 0 MISSED but full tally was never obtained).
CalendarDate lacking Ord was not documented in ars-i18n or the spec — discovered only at compile time when BTreeSet<CalendarDate> was rejected.
Callback::new HRTB limitation was not documented; discovered at compile time when porting IsDateUnavailableFn, requiring a detour to add new_ref to ars-core.
Bulk-replacing let _ = svc.send(...) with drop(...) required manual sed attempts that produced backup files and needed cleanup — no project-wide sed helper exists.
Spec §1.8 hover events referenced fields and events not defined in §1.2, causing confusion about whether they were intentional or drift — required user confirmation.
open_items: Shift+Space range-from-anchor and Ctrl+A select-all multi-select keyboard interactions are not implemented — requires an 'anchor' concept to be defined in a spec follow-up before implementation.
HoverDate/HoverEnd events and range context fields (is_range, range_start, range_end) are explicitly deferred to the future RangeCalendar component.
Full cargo mutants run was never completed; partial runs (47 + 112 outcomes) showed 0 MISSED but a clean end-to-end tally was not obtained.
snapshot-reviewed GitHub label and waiting-for-codex-review skill invocation are listed in the workflow but were not confirmed as done in the transcript after PR creation.

Callback::new_ref is the first HRTB Callback constructor in the workspace — other components with predicate Props (is_date_unavailable pattern) could migrate from fn pointers to Callback using this new API.
